ogTitle: Baw Baw vs Bawley Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Baw Baw and Bawley Point.
heading: Baw Baw vs Bawley Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Baw Baw, New South Wales and Bawley Point, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Bawley Point has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Baw Baw (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Baw Baw or Bawley Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Baw Baw has a flood risk score of 29/100, while Bawley Point scores 32/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Baw Baw scores 56/100 for bushfire risk. Bawley Point scores 43/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Baw Baw or Bawley Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Bawley Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
